req.title: Create medication request (audit)
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-083 mandates three distinct verification axes: (1) a prescriber with valid credentials can create a non-controlled MedicationRequest and receive 201 + AuditEvent (positive/EP); (2) a prescriber attempting a CII controlled-substance request WITHOUT schedule verification is rejected with 403 (negative/NEG+DT); (3) the state boundary where schedule verification transitions from absent to present for a CII drug unlocks creation — tested at the exact decision boundary (boundary/BVA+STC). A fourth test covers the AuditEvent emission end-to-end for a successful request, exercising the full state transition from request-submitted to audit-recorded (STC). Together these cases achieve EP partition coverage (valid/invalid actor, controlled/non-controlled drug, verified/unverified schedule), BVA on the schedule-verification flag, STC on the resource lifecycle, DT on the controlled-substance decision rules, and NEG on the 403 pathway.

tests.name: test_prescriber_creates_noncontrolled_medication_request
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with valid prescriber JWT and a non-controlled medication payload
Capture HTTP response status and Location header
GET the returned Location URL to verify resource stored
expected: Resource id is present and non-empty
Pharmacy notification flag is true in response meta or side-channel
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_prescriber_creates_noncontrolled_medication_request(
    prescriber_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                 # <fixture>
    noncontrolled_med_payload # <fixture>
):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
        json=noncontrolled_med_payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "MedicationRequest"
    resource_id = body.get("id")
    assert resource_id, "Resource id must be present"

    location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
    fetch = requests.get(location, headers=prescriber_auth_headers)
    assert fetch.status_code == 200
    assert fetch.json().get("id") == resource_id

    pharmacy_notified = body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])
    assert any(t.get("code") == "pharmacy-notified" for t in pharmacy_notified), \
        "Pharmacy notification tag missing"

tests.name: test_cii_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403
preconditions: Target medication is Schedule II controlled substance
Prescriber session has NO schedule-verification token/claim
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with valid prescriber JWT but NO schedule-verification header/claim
Payload specifies a CII controlled substance (e.g. oxycodone)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code schedule-verification-required
No MedicationRequest resource is created in the system
An AuditEvent of type 110110 (access-denied) is emitted
trace: R-HLT-083: error pathway — 403 without schedule verification for CII
R-HLT-083: controlled substances require schedule verification
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_cii_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403(
    prescriber_auth_headers,   # <fixture> — no schedule-verification claim
    base_url,                  # <fixture>
    cii_med_payload,           # <fixture> — oxycodone, scheduleCode=II
    audit_event_client         # <fixture>
):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
        json=cii_med_payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    issue = body.get("issue", [{}])[0]
    assert issue.get("details", {}).get("coding", [{}])[0].get("code") == \
        "schedule-verification-required"

    events = audit_event_client.query(action="C", outcome="8")  # <helper_fn>
    assert any(
        e.get("type", {}).get("code") == "110110" for e in events
    ), "Access-denied AuditEvent not found"

tests.name: test_cii_request_with_schedule_verification_succeeds_boundary
preconditions: Target medication is Schedule II controlled substance
Prescriber session EXACTLY has a valid schedule-verification token (minimum required claim present)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with prescriber JWT that includes schedule-verification claim set to true (boundary: just-sufficient verification)
Payload specifies same CII controlled substance as negative test
Capture HTTP status, resource id, and AuditEvent
expected: AuditEvent of type 110100 (successful create) is emitted with agent matching prescriber id
trace: R-HLT-083: controlled substances require schedule verification — at-boundary pass
R-HLT-083: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_cii_request_with_schedule_verification_succeeds_boundary(
    prescriber_auth_headers_with_schedule_verification,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                                            # <fixture>
    cii_med_payload,                                     # <fixture>
    audit_event_client,                                  # <fixture>
    prescriber_id                                        # <fixture>
):
    ts_before = int(time.time())
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
        json=cii_med_payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers_with_schedule_verification
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "MedicationRequest"
    resource_id = body.get("id")
    assert resource_id, "Resource id must be non-empty"

    events = audit_event_client.query_since(ts_before, action="C", outcome="0")  # <helper_fn>
    matching = [
        e for e in events
        if e.get("type", {}).get("code") == "110100"
        and any(a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "").endswith(prescriber_id)
                for a in e.get("agent", []))
    ]
    assert matching, "Success AuditEvent with prescriber agent not found"

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_for_every_medication_request_operation
preconditions: AuditEvent store is empty or baseline count is captured before test
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Capture current AuditEvent count for this prescriber
Assert AuditEvent count increased by exactly 2
expected: AuditEvent count increases by exactly 2 (one per operation)
Each AuditEvent references the correct MedicationRequest resource id
AuditEvents have distinct action codes C (create) and R (read)
trace: R-HLT-083: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
R-HLT-083: state transition — submitted -> stored -> retrieved
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_audit_event_emitted_for_every_medication_request_operation(
    prescriber_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                 # <fixture>
    noncontrolled_med_payload,# <fixture>
    audit_event_client,       # <fixture>
    prescriber_id             # <fixture>
):
    before_count = audit_event_client.count_for_agent(prescriber_id)  # <helper_fn>

    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
        json=noncontrolled_med_payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    resource_id = post_resp.json()["id"]

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest/{resource_id}",
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200

    after_count = audit_event_client.count_for_agent(prescriber_id)  # <helper_fn>
    assert after_count - before_count == 2, \
        f"Expected 2 new AuditEvents, got {after_count - before_count}"

    events = audit_event_client.query_for_entity(resource_id)  # <helper_fn>
    action_codes = {e.get("action") for e in events}
    assert "C" in action_codes, "Create AuditEvent missing"
    assert "R" in action_codes, "Read AuditEvent missing"
